title: petrosaviales
intro: The rank petrosaviales is a clade of flowering plants, specifically an order. It is a distinct grouping of plant families that share common characteristics and evolutionary relationships. The petrosaviales order includes families such as Orchidaceae, which comprises orchids, Euphorbiaceae, which includes spurge and poinsettia, and Cyperaceae, which includes sedges and rushes. These families are thought to have evolved from a common ancestor and share unique traits that distinguish them from other flowering plant groups.

families.family_desc: Petrosaviaceae is a family of flowering plants that are primitive, leafless, and lacking in true roots and seeds, with a unique, highly modified morphology.
